# General Simulation

> General Simulation predicts which version of a product change will win, who abandons, and why, before a single real user sees it.

## What it is

General Simulation is a simulated-user experimentation platform. Instead of waiting weeks for a live A/B test to reach significance, teams learn up front which variant is likely to win, the expected completion and drop-off for each option, and the reasons behind them.

## What it does

- Predicts the winning variant of an experiment before it ships.
- Estimates completion and abandonment for each option.
- Surfaces where users struggle and why they leave.
- Backed by blind backtests on real experiments with known outcomes. On a real 64,000-customer experiment it was 94% accurate at picking the winning variant, versus 35% when the underlying behavioral signal is scrambled, a control that shows the accuracy comes from real signal, not chance or memorization. Results are published where the method works and where it doesn't.

## Who it's for

Growth, product, and CRO (conversion rate optimization) teams who want to de-risk experiments and prioritize the changes most likely to win before committing engineering and live traffic.

## Key facts

- Category: simulated-user testing / experimentation / conversion optimization.
- Differentiator: predictions are validated by blind backtests on real experiments, not guesswork.
- Status: early access.

## Links

- Home and early access: https://generalsimulation.com
